body.title: Kill switch: authenticate halt/block/resume caller via provenance token, not self-reported string
body.result: provenance token required for agent_halt/resume + device_halt/block; 7 tests
body.description:
CRITICAL gap found in T-murderbot-security-review.

skeleton._check_caller_auth() trusts the self-reported from_device string. Any caller that passes from_device='skeleton' passes the auth check for halt and block. This defeats the kill switch: a prompt-injected agent can halt any device, or resume itself after being halted, by claiming from_device='skeleton'.

Current code in skeleton.py _add_device_control_tools:
  def device_halt(from_device: str) -> dict:
      skel._check_caller_auth(from_device, did, 'halt')
      # from_device is a string the caller supplies -- unverified

Fix: halt, block, and resume tools must verify the caller via the provenance registry. The caller supplies their active provenance token; skeleton calls ProvenanceService.verify() before allowing the operation. The existing code comment says 'localhost trust; crypto ACL Phase 5+' -- this is the upgrade. Must ship before or with T-agent-kill-switch.

**Affected files:** unseen_university/skeleton/skeleton.py (_check_caller_auth, _add_device_control_tools)
**Design rules:** BaseDevice/BaseShim; flat-file registry; ProvenanceService.verify() is the gate
**Scope boundary:** IN -- token-backed auth for halt/block; OUT -- full PKI, cross-rack ACL
**Completion criteria:** calling {device_id}_halt without a valid provenance token is denied; a prompt-injected agent cannot halt a device by self-reporting from_device='skeleton'; tests cover the auth rejection path
